content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a literal "TODO" placeholder (App/Info.plist), and NSCameraUsageDescription is generic boilerplate not tied to the actual document-scanning feature seen in ScannerView.swift.
Pierre: Apple's reviewers reject purpose strings that are placeholders or too generic, since they must clearly explain to the user why the app needs the permission and for what feature. "TODO" will almost certainly trigger a rejection, and there's no visible feature in the code using location at all, so its necessity should be reconsidered or removed. Fix the camera string to reference document scanning specifically (e.g., "SnapScan uses the camera to scan and digitize your documents") and either implement/justify the location usage or delete that key entirely.
